moat.description: A global duopoly with Moody's in debt ratings. Regulatory and brand moat.
moat.analysisSummary: S&P Global operates a **Financial Toll Bridge**:

analysisPoints.title: Regulatory Oligopoly
analysisPoints.text: You cannot issue global debt without a rating from S&P or Moody's. It is a legally-embedded requirement for institutional investors.

analysisPoints.title: IP Moat
analysisPoints.text: The S&P 500 brand is the most licensed index in the world. Asset managers pay SPGI every time a new ETF is created.

analysisPoints.title: Low Capex
analysisPoints.text: Once the rating methodologies and data platforms are built, every additional dollar of revenue flows straight to the bottom line.

growth.description: Q1 2026 total revenue +10% YoY ($4.17B), above the 6-8% organic guidance midpoint. Indices led at +17% (ETF AUM tailwind); Ratings +13% as the refinancing wall drives issuance. Adjusted EPS +13.7%, and FY26 adjusted-EPS guidance was reaffirmed at $19.40-19.65. Energy (Platts/Commodity Insights) organic guidance was trimmed ~1pp (to 4.5-6%) on Iran-conflict energy disruption. From July 1, 2026 the Mobility segment departs in the MBGL spin-off, leaving a faster-growing ratings/indices/market-intelligence RemainCo.

growthAnalysis.scoreDerivation: Base 72 + duopoly ratings position (+4) + indices ETF AUM tailwind (+3) + Platts commodities optionality (+2) - issuance cyclicality risk (-1) = 80

drivers.name: Ratings
drivers.metric: Q1 2026 Ratings revenue +13% YoY on refinancing wall; private credit ratings revenue +25% YoY

growthAnalysis.keyRisk: Tariff-driven debt capital markets freeze in 2026 could halt new bond issuance for 6-12 months and compress MIS revenue.

valuation.description: At $424.42 (June 26, 2026) the stock trades ~6% above the bear-case target ($400) and ~24% of the way from bear ($400) to base ($500) — most of the cyclical uncertainty already appears discounted, with Q1 2026 beating guidance and Ratings private-credit revenue +25% YoY. Spin-off timing: the Mobility Global (MBGL) spin-off distributes July 1, 2026 (record date June 15; $2B of Mobility notes priced in May). June 26 is the last regular-way cum-distribution session, so from July 1 the SPGI stub re-prices mechanically lower as MBGL (estimated ~$25-33/share) separates. These bear/base/bull targets are struck on the pre-spin whole company and must be rebased to RemainCo after the distribution. Post-spin, SPGI becomes a purer ratings/indices/data compounder, which has historically supported a higher multiple.

bear.description: Tariff-driven uncertainty freezes debt capital markets activity, ESG backlash reduces data segment revenue, and multiple compresses.
points: Tariff and geopolitical uncertainty freezes new corporate bond issuance for 6-12 months
ESG/sustainability data revenue faces continued political and institutional backlash in US market
Financial sector consolidation reduces Market Intelligence terminal and data subscription counts

base.description: The refinancing wall drives sustained ratings demand through 2026-2027, with index licensing and private markets expanding the revenue mix.
points: Refinancing wall of $10T+ in corporate debt drives steady ratings demand through 2027
Index licensing grows 12-15% annually as ETF AUM expansion continues globally
Private credit ratings become a standard requirement as the $1.5T+ market matures

bull.description: Private market data becomes a category-defining business, and AI-powered market intelligence creates a new high-growth revenue stream.
points: Private credit and private equity data analytics become a $1B+ revenue segment
AI-powered Market Intelligence platform commands significant pricing power premium
Dividend growth re-accelerates to 15%+ as FCF generation exceeds $5B annually

learnedInterfaces.note: S&P credit rating nomenclature (AAA, AA+, etc.) is embedded in trillions of dollars of fixed-income legal documents — ISDA master agreements, fund investment mandates, Basel III capital rules, and money-market eligibility rules reference S&P ratings explicitly. Replacing this learned interface across the global fixed-income complex would require amending those documents and retraining every fixed-income professional. AI-powered analytics platforms layer on top of S&P ratings rather than replacing them.

businessLogic.note: Credit rating methodologies are proprietary, regulatory-recognized, and legally required — AI enhances but cannot replace the NRSRO designation.

publicDataAccess.note: S&P aggregates decades of public filings, Platts commodity assessments, and ESG disclosures into proprietary datasets that took 20+ years and multiple acquisitions (IHS Markit, Kensho, Panjiva) to assemble. The mobility/auto dataset departs with the Mobility Global spin-off (distribution July 1, 2026), but the core ratings history, Platts benchmarks, and Capital IQ corpus — now also being licensed into enterprise AI platforms (Cohere North collaboration, June 2026) — remain unique and non-replicable on a near-term horizon.

talentScarcity.note: SEC-recognized credit analysts, regulatory relations specialists, and 160-year institutional knowledge cannot be replicated.

bundling.note: Credit Ratings + Market Intelligence (Capital IQ) + Platts commodity assessments + Mobility data + Indices form a deeply integrated bundle — institutional users who rely on multiple S&P products face compounding switching costs because replacing the bundle requires sourcing each component from different vendors offering inferior standalone products.

proprietaryData.note: 160 years of credit ratings history, Platts energy commodity benchmarks, and proprietary financial data — legally embedded in markets.

regulatoryLockIn.note: SEC-recognized NRSRO status is a legal moat. Replicating this designation requires decades of track record and regulatory approval.

networkEffects.note: Credit ratings are network-critical — bond issuers MUST use NRSRO-recognized agencies; investors MUST reference them.

transactionEmbedding.note: S&P ratings are legally embedded in every major bond covenant, loan agreement, regulatory filing, and pension fund mandate.

systemOfRecord.note: The authoritative system of record for global credit risk — no alternative source carries the same legal and institutional weight.

tenMoats.verdict: S&P Global's regulatory moat (NRSRO status) and role as the definitive system of record for credit risk makes them uniquely AI-resilient. AI disrupts analysis, not the legal requirement to use S&P ratings.
